Dholpur (Vindhyan) sandstone — the country's red-stone benchmark (Red Fort, Rashtrapati Bhavan).
Karauli sandstone cluster — sculpting-grade for temple construction.
Khetri (Jhunjhunu) hosts India's largest copper resource — Hindustan Copper Ltd operates the belt since 1967.
Sub-clusters: Khetri, Kolihan, Chandmari, Banwas mines and the Khetri smelter.
Belongs to the Delhi Supergroup — copper mineralisation in Proterozoic quartzites and metasediments.
Ramganj Mandi (Kota) — India's premier Kota Stone dimension-limestone quarrying cluster.
Feeds Rajasthan's cement industry (ACC Lakheri in Bundi, Ambuja Chittorgarh) via limestone leases.
Baran-Kota Vindhyan limestone-sandstone belt exports ~500 kt of dimension stone annually.
Makrana (Nagaur) supplies India's white marble benchmark — the Taj Mahal stone.
Kishangarh (Ajmer) is India's largest marble mandi — market for polished slabs and blocks.
Marble mining is regulated under Rajasthan's Marble Development and Conservation Rules 2002.
Udaipur zinc-phosphate cluster — Zawar (world's oldest zinc smelter site, 12th c.), Rajpura-Dariba, Jhamarkotra rock phosphate.
Hindustan Zinc Ltd (Vedanta) — India's only zinc producer, largely operating from Rajasthan.
RSMML runs the Jhamarkotra rock phosphate mine (~90% of India's mined output).
Notified 1960 — protects Rajasthan's only montane subtropical semi-evergreen patch on the Aravalli's highest ridge.
Habitat of the endemic Mount Abu skink (Eurylepis moerella) and Rajasthan's only leopard-native evergreen belt.
Contains Guru Shikhar (1722 m), Rajasthan's highest peak, and the Dilwara Jain temples.
extraFacts_hi: 1960 में अधिसूचित — अरावली की सर्वोच्च कटक पर राजस्थान के एकमात्र पर्वतीय उपोष्ण अर्ध-सदाबहार खंड की रक्षा करता है।
स्थानिक माउंट आबू स्किंक (Eurylepis moerella) का आवास और राजस्थान की एकमात्र तेंदुआ-मूल सदाबहार पेटी।
इसमें गुरु शिखर (1722 मी), राजस्थान की सर्वोच्च चोटी, और दिलवाड़ा जैन मंदिर हैं।

extraFacts: Notified as a National Park in 2006 and Tiger Reserve in 2013 — Rajasthan's third Tiger Reserve.
Combines Darrah, Chambal and Jawahar Sagar sanctuaries into a single tiger landscape.
The Chambal, Kali Sindh, Ahu, Parbati and Bamani rivers all cut through the park — India's densest river network in a Tiger Reserve.
extraFacts_hi: 2006 में राष्ट्रीय उद्यान और 2013 में टाइगर रिज़र्व अधिसूचित — राजस्थान का तीसरा टाइगर रिज़र्व।
दर्रा, चंबल और जवाहर सागर अभयारण्यों को एक ही बाघ-भूदृश्य में जोड़ता है।
चंबल, कालीसिंध, आहू, पार्वती और बामनी — सभी नदियाँ उद्यान से होकर बहती हैं; किसी टाइगर रिज़र्व में भारत का सघनतम नदी-तंत्र।

extraFacts: Notified 2013 as Rajasthan's third Tiger Reserve.
Named 'Dara' (the pass) after the Kota kings' private hunting reserve.
The Chambal cuts through the reserve — critical gharial and mugger habitat.
extraFacts_hi: 2013 में राजस्थान के तीसरे टाइगर रिज़र्व के रूप में अधिसूचित।
नाम 'दर्रा' (घाटी-मार्ग) पर, जो कोटा नरेशों का निजी शिकार-आरक्षण था।
चंबल रिज़र्व से होकर बहती है — घड़ियाल और मगर का महत्त्वपूर्ण आवास।

extraFacts: Historic capital of the Naga Rajputs and later the Khanjada Nagauris; Nagaur Fort (12th c., extended by Rao Amar Singh Rathore in the 17th c.) is one of only two Rajput forts on a plain (with Junagarh Bikaner).
Nagaur cattle fair — held in Jan–Feb on the Bhilwara–Nagaur road — is Asia's second-largest livestock fair (after Pushkar).
Home to the Bagdari village in Nagaur where Panchayati Raj was inaugurated in India on 2 October 1959 by Jawaharlal Nehru.
Merta City (Nagaur) is Mirabai's birthplace; also site of India's first rail-bus service (2 October 1994, Merta Road → Merta City).
Central Sheep & Wool Research Institute (Avikanagar, Malpura — technically Tonk, but adjacent to Nagaur) established 1962.
Gotan (Merta tahsil) hosts Rajasthan's first white cement factory; the district also anchors the 'Panch Pyare' Marwari dryland cropping tradition.
Ring sanctuary around Nahargarh Fort north of Jaipur — Rajasthan's newest formally-notified WLS boundary (1980, refined 2019).
Biological Park at Nahargarh houses the state's ex-situ programmes for Asiatic lion, tiger, panther and vulture.
Buffer forest between Amber Fort–Jaigarh landscape and the Aravalli tail into Alwar.
extraFacts_hi: जयपुर के उत्तर में नाहरगढ़ दुर्ग के चारों ओर वलय अभयारण्य — राजस्थान की सबसे नई औपचारिक अभयारण्य सीमा (1980, 2019 में परिष्कृत)।
नाहरगढ़ का जैविक उद्यान एशियाई सिंह, बाघ, तेंदुआ और गिद्ध के लिए राज्य के बाह्य-स्थल कार्यक्रम चलाता है।
आमेर दुर्ग–जयगढ़ भूदृश्य और अलवर तक जाती अरावली पूँछ के बीच बफ़र वन।

extraFacts: Highest lake in Rajasthan (~1200 m) — the only cirque-type basin in the Aravalli, at Mount Abu (Sirohi).
Legend: gouged out by the nails ('nakh') of the gods; Toad Rock, Nun Rock, Camel Rock frame its rim.
Portions of Mahatma Gandhi's ashes were immersed here in 1948 — marked by Gandhi Ghat.
Tri-state sanctuary (Rajasthan–MP–UP) notified 1979 — protects 400+ km of the Chambal in Kota, Sawai Madhopur, Karauli and Dholpur.
Holds India's largest wild population of the critically-endangered gharial (Gavialis gangeticus).
Also the northernmost breeding site of the Ganges river dolphin (Platanista gangetica) and the smooth-coated otter.
extraFacts_hi: त्रि-राज्यीय अभयारण्य (राजस्थान–म.प्र.–उ.प्र.), 1979 में अधिसूचित — कोटा, सवाई माधोपुर, करौली और धौलपुर में चंबल के 400+ किमी की रक्षा करता है।
अति-संकटग्रस्त घड़ियाल (Gavialis gangeticus) की भारत की सबसे बड़ी वन्य आबादी यहाँ है।
गंगा नदी डॉल्फ़िन (Platanista gangetica) और चिकनी ऊदबिलाव का सबसे उत्तरी प्रजनन स्थल भी।

extraFacts: 'Textile-dyeing capital of Rajasthan' — Pali–Sojat is India's densest cluster of red-mud (Sojat mehndi henna) and cotton bandhani dye units.
Ranakpur Jain temple complex (Pali) is one of Jainism's five holiest tirthas — 1444 uniquely-carved marble columns; built 1439 under Rana Kumbha.
Jawai Dam (Pali–Sirohi border) is western Rajasthan's largest reservoir; the surrounding Jawai leopard landscape hosts India's densest unfenced leopard population.
Jadan (Pali) is the site of the world's first Om Ashram — a 250 ft Om-shaped Shiva sanctuary complex.
Sojat mehndi (Pali) has GI status — Rajasthan's highest-quality henna, exported worldwide.
Left-bank tributary of the Chambal, forming the Rajasthan–MP boundary along part of Baran district.
Meets the Chambal at Palighat (Baran) after a course of ~437 km overall.
The Parbati–Kalisindh–Chambal (PKC) link project on this river is one of India's oldest-planned inter-basin transfers.
A right-bank tributary of the Kalisindh in the Hadoti plateau.
Rises in the Malwa plateau in MP; enters Rajasthan in Jhalawar district.
The Parvan Multipurpose Project (announced 2018) is designed to irrigate ~2 lakh hectares across Jhalawar, Baran and Kota.
Carved out of Jodhpur on 7 August 2023.
Recorded India's highest reliably-measured air temperature — 51.0°C on 19 May 2016 — as the country's hottest place.
Khichan village (Phalodi) hosts the annual demoiselle crane (Grus virgo) migration — Asia's largest concentration, ~20,000 birds each winter (Nov-Mar).
The Salt Belt of Kaparda–Bap–Phalodi anchors Rajasthan's arid-zone inland salt production.
Site of the Rajasthan cabinet's 'Phalodi Satta Bazaar' — India's oldest informal political betting market.
Notified 1983 — 512 sq km along the southern Aravalli in Udaipur district, part of the Sitamata–Kumbhalgarh corridor.
'Phulwari ki Nal' = 'valley of flowers' — highest floral diversity of any Rajasthan sanctuary.
Bhagoria (mahua-flower) collection by the resident Kathodia and Garasia tribes is regulated within the sanctuary.
extraFacts_hi: 1983 में अधिसूचित — उदयपुर जिले में दक्षिणी अरावली के साथ 512 वर्ग किमी, सीतामाता–कुंभलगढ़ गलियारे का भाग।
'फुलवारी की नाल' = 'फूलों की घाटी' — राजस्थान के किसी भी अभयारण्य से अधिक पुष्प-विविधता।
निवासी कथौड़ी और गरासिया जनजातियों द्वारा भगोरिया (महुआ-फूल) संग्रह अभयारण्य के भीतर विनियमित है।

extraFacts: Artificial freshwater lake commissioned by a Banjara chief in 1362, expanded under Maharana Udai Singh in 1560.
Houses Jag Mandir and Jag Niwas (now Taj Lake Palace) on its islands.
Basin: Berach → Chambal. Fed by the Sisarma river and monsoon runoff from surrounding hills.
In September 1997 hosted the world's first solar-powered boat — a Ministry of Non-Conventional Energy demo.
Artificial freshwater lake at Udaipur — expanded 1560 by Maharana Udai Singh.
Fed by the Sisarma river; drains to Berach → Chambal.
Habitat for painted stork, pond heron, kingfishers; ornithological hot-spot in monsoon.
extraFacts_hi: उदयपुर की कृत्रिम मीठे पानी की झील — 1560 में महाराणा उदय सिंह द्वारा विस्तारित।
सीसारमा नदी से पोषित; बेड़च → चंबल में जल-निकास।
जांघिल, अंधा बगुला और किलकिला का आवास; मानसून में पक्षी-विज्ञान का हॉटस्पॉट।

extraFacts: RAPS-1 was India's second nuclear reactor (after Tarapur), with design help from Canada (BARC/AECL).
Uses Rana Pratap Sagar reservoir water for cooling — hence the site name Rawatbhata.
RAPS 7-8 (700 MW × 2 indigenous PHWRs) under construction — will take Rawatbhata past 2.5 GW.
Rajasthan's newest full district before the 2023 wave — carved from Chittorgarh, Banswara and Udaipur on 26 January 2008.
Highest average annual rainfall in Rajasthan (~900 mm) — the district sits at the 90 cm isohyet.
Thewa jewellery — 24-carat gold-on-glass filigree — is a GI-tagged craft unique to Pratapgarh (Raj Sonis' 400-year-old technique).
Sitamata Wildlife Sanctuary (Pratapgarh) hosts India's densest population of Indian flying squirrel (Petaurista philippensis) and the Rajasthan state animal, chinkara.
Pratapgarh anchors the southern-most Bhil-Meena tribal transition belt of Rajasthan.
Sacred lake — India's only continuously worshipped Brahma-associated freshwater tirth.
One of Rajasthan's few 'natural' lake basins (though embanked by Ajmer rulers).
Hosts the world's biggest camel fair (Kartik Purnima, Oct-Nov) — 25,000+ camels traded annually.
52 ghats and 500+ temples ring the lake — Rajasthan's densest sacred architecture.
extraFacts_hi: पवित्र झील — भारत का एकमात्र निरंतर पूजित ब्रह्मा-संबद्ध मीठे पानी का तीर्थ।
राजस्थान के गिने-चुने 'प्राकृतिक' झील-द्रोणियों में से एक (यद्यपि अजमेर शासकों ने तटबंध बनवाए)।
विश्व का सबसे बड़ा ऊँट मेला यहीं लगता है (कार्तिक पूर्णिमा, अक्टूबर-नवंबर) — प्रतिवर्ष 25,000+ ऊँटों का व्यापार।
झील के चारों ओर 52 घाट और 500+ मंदिर — राजस्थान की सघनतम पवित्र स्थापत्य।

extraFacts: Barmer, Jodhpur, Nagaur, Churu, Hanumangarh — the outer arid belt.
Bajra, moth, guar in kharif; barley + gram in rabi under limited well irrigation.
Wind erosion is the primary land-degradation risk here.
Jalore, Pali, Didwana-Kuchaman, Sikar, Jhunjhunu — the semi-arid heart of Rajasthan.
Bajra + mustard + gram rotation; groundnut cluster in Shekhawati.
Groundwater draft is India's highest in this zone.
Udaipur, Rajsamand, Bhilwara, Ajmer, Jaipur, Kotputli-Behror — the Aravalli piedmont.
Wheat + mustard + gram in rabi; jowar + bajra + pulses in kharif.
Bisalpur command area supplies part of this zone via the Banas.
Dungarpur, Udaipur south, Chittorgarh, Bhilwara south, Bundi, Sawai Madhopur, Karauli, Dholpur.
Mustard–wheat + soybean+wheat rotations; the Hadoti plateau anchors here.
The tribal maize belt sits at the humid edge of this zone.
Only Jhalawar, Banswara and Pratapgarh cross 900+ mm; Pratapgarh often exceeds 1000 mm in monsoon.
The state's highest rainfall recorded is on the 90 cm isohyet of the Malwa plateau.
Enables Rajasthan's densest natural forest cover (Sitamata WLS, Kumbhalgarh WLS).
Rainfall so low that the Thar functions as India's only 'true' desert (Köppen BWh).
Districts: Jaisalmer, Phalodi, western Bikaner, southern Sri Ganganagar; annual rainfall 100-200 mm.
Evaporation exceeds 1500 mm annually — 7-8× the rainfall.
Kumbhalgarh Fort (Rajsamand) has the world's second-longest continuous wall after China — 36 km encircling 13 hill-tops (UNESCO Hill Forts of Rajasthan, 2013).
Rajsamand Lake (built 1662-1676 by Maharana Raj Singh I) is 6.4 km long — one of the largest artificial lakes of its era.
The Raj Prashasti (24 stone slabs, ~1017 verses) on the lake's Nauchowki embankment is the world's largest Sanskrit inscription.
Nathdwara (Rajsamand) is the seat of the Pushti Marg sect and hosts Shrinathji temple — the world's largest Shiva statue 'Vishwa Swaroopam' (110 m) was opened here in 2022.
The Kankroli-Rajsamand marble belt — with Rajsamand and Kishangarh — supplies over 60% of India's marble output; Makrana (Nagaur) supplies the white-benchmark variety.
Built 1662-1676 by Maharana Raj Singh I of Mewar; ~6.4 km long, one of the largest artificial lakes of its era.
Nine-pavilion embankment (Nauchowki) carries the Raj Prashasti — the world's largest Sanskrit inscription (24 slabs, ~1017 verses by Ranchhod Bhatt).
Basin: Gomti River → Banas → Chambal. The lake was built to relieve famine — a labour-for-food project.
Historic Jaipur artificial lake (built 1902) that supplied the city's drinking water for decades.
Hosted the sailing and rowing events of the 1982 Asian Games — the only Asian Games venue in Rajasthan.
Ran dry after the 2000s due to catchment encroachment; Supreme Court-mandated restoration underway.
Jaipur's historic drinking-water lake (built 1902); the 1982 Asian Games sailing and rowing venue.
Now largely dry — the catchment has been encroached and the lake's Rajasthan HC-ordered restoration is ongoing.
Encircled by the Jamwa Ramgarh Wildlife Sanctuary.
extraFacts_hi: जयपुर की ऐतिहासिक पेयजल झील (1902 में निर्मित); 1982 एशियाई खेलों का नौकायन और रोइंग स्थल।
अब लगभग सूखी — जल-ग्रहण क्षेत्र पर अतिक्रमण हुआ; राजस्थान उच्च न्यायालय के आदेश पर पुनर्स्थापन जारी है।
जमवा रामगढ़ वन्यजीव अभयारण्य से घिरी।

extraFacts: Notified 2022 as India's 52nd and Rajasthan's fourth Tiger Reserve.
Ramgarh Vishdhari WLS (Bundi, 1982) forms the reserve's core.
Corridor between Ranthambore TR (north) and Mukundra Hills TR (south) — critical for tiger dispersal.
extraFacts_hi: 2022 में भारत के 52वें और राजस्थान के चौथे टाइगर रिज़र्व के रूप में अधिसूचित।
रामगढ़ विषधारी अभयारण्य (बूँदी, 1982) रिज़र्व का कोर बनाता है।
रणथंभौर (उत्तर) और मुकुंदरा हिल्स (दक्षिण) के बीच गलियारा — बाघों के फैलाव के लिए महत्त्वपूर्ण।

extraFacts: Notified 1982 (Bundi); upgraded to India's 52nd Tiger Reserve in 2022.
Wildlife corridor between Ranthambore TR (north) and Mukundra Hills TR (south) — critical for tiger dispersal.
Hosts one of Rajasthan's healthiest sambar and chital populations; leopards were the dominant predator before tiger reintroduction.
extraFacts_hi: 1982 में अधिसूचित (बूँदी); 2022 में भारत के 52वें टाइगर रिज़र्व में उन्नत।
रणथंभौर (उत्तर) और मुकुंदरा हिल्स (दक्षिण) के बीच वन्यजीव गलियारा — बाघों के फैलाव के लिए महत्त्वपूर्ण।
राजस्थान की सबसे स्वस्थ सांभर और चीतल आबादियों में से एक; बाघ-पुनर्वास से पहले तेंदुआ यहाँ प्रमुख परभक्षी था।

extraFacts: Ramsagar Lake sanctuary (Dholpur) — notified 1955 as one of Rajasthan's oldest sanctuaries.
Winter refuge for painted stork, greylag goose, black-necked stork and Sarus crane.
Buffer to the National Chambal Sanctuary — southern edge of the Ravines gharial habitat.
extraFacts_hi: रामसागर झील अभयारण्य (धौलपुर) — 1955 में राजस्थान के प्राचीनतम अभयारण्यों में से एक के रूप में अधिसूचित।
जांघिल, ग्रेलैग हंस, ब्लैक-नेक्ड स्टॉर्क और सारस की शीतकालीन शरण।
राष्ट्रीय चंबल अभयारण्य का बफ़र — बीहड़ों के घड़ियाल आवास का दक्षिणी छोर।

extraFacts: Rajasthan's first National Park (1980) and India's first Tiger Reserve under Project Tiger (1973).
Ranthambore Fort (10th c., Chauhan) inside the park is a UNESCO Hill Fort of Rajasthan.
Home to Trinetra Ganesh temple (the fort's ancient Ganesh shrine).
extraFacts_hi: राजस्थान का पहला राष्ट्रीय उद्यान (1980) और Project Tiger के अंतर्गत भारत का पहला टाइगर रिज़र्व (1973)।
उद्यान के भीतर रणथंभौर दुर्ग (10वीं शताब्दी, चौहान) UNESCO की 'राजस्थान के पहाड़ी दुर्ग' सूची में शामिल है।
यहाँ त्रिनेत्र गणेश मंदिर है — दुर्ग का प्राचीन गणेश स्थल।

extraFacts: India's first Tiger Reserve (1973, Project Tiger's flagship site).
One of India's most successful tiger recovery landscapes — supplied the founder stock for Sariska (2008) reintroduction.
The Ranthambore Fort within the reserve is a UNESCO Hill Fort of Rajasthan.
extraFacts_hi: भारत का पहला टाइगर रिज़र्व (1973, Project Tiger का प्रमुख स्थल)।
भारत के सर्वाधिक सफल बाघ-पुनर्प्राप्ति भूदृश्यों में से एक — सरिस्का पुनर्वास (2008) के लिए संस्थापक बाघ यहीं से गए।
रिज़र्व के भीतर रणथंभौर दुर्ग UNESCO की 'राजस्थान के पहाड़ी दुर्ग' सूची में है।

extraFacts: Largest cultural region of Rajasthan — anchored on Jodhpur, the former Rathore capital.
Marwari dialect and cuisine (dal-baati-churma) are the region's cultural markers.
Godwar is a distinct sub-region at the Aravalli piedmont — Ranakpur temple + Jawai reservoir.
Home to the world's largest open-air fresco gallery — painted havelis of Nawalgarh, Mandawa, Fatehpur.
Historic birthplace of the Marwari trading diaspora — Birla, Bajaj, Poddar, Goenka, Jhunjhunwala.
Distinctive dialect (Shekhawati) — a variant of Marwari with Braj influence.
Wind-borne sand cover of the Thar — Quaternary, ~2 Ma old.
Dune types: longitudinal (linear), star (multi-armed), barchan (crescent) — largest reach 150 m in western Jaisalmer.
Rajasthan holds India's largest aeolian cover (~2 lakh sq km).
Quaternary river-borne sediments of the Banas, Chambal, Yamuna, Ghaggar basins.
Highly fertile — supports the state's densest cropping in eastern plains + Ganganagar canal belt.
Palaeo-Ghaggar (Vedic Saraswati) alluvium at Kalibangan is Rajasthan's most-studied buried river deposit.
Two flagship intrusions: Malani Igneous Suite (750 Ma, Barmer–Jodhpur; anorogenic rhyolite–granite) and Erinpura Granite (~800 Ma, Sirohi–Udaipur).
Deccan Trap basalt (66 Ma) enters at the SE edge in Jhalawar–Banswara.
Together underlie ~15% of Rajasthan's mapped rock area.
Aravalli SG phyllites, schists, marbles; Delhi SG quartzites, metasediments; Bhilwara SG gneisses and granulites.
Highest-grade metamorphics in Rajasthan — Sandmata Complex (Bhilwara SG) granulites, ~1.8 Ga.
Metamorphism drove the marble belt: Bilara limestone → Makrana marble; Aravalli SG limestones → Ambaji/Kumbalgarh marble.
Marwar SG (~600 Ma, Ediacaran-Cambrian) — sandstone-limestone-shale sequence covering western Rajasthan.
Vindhyan SG (~1000-600 Ma) — Kota Stone (limestone), Dholpur sandstone, Karauli sandstone.
Together underlie ~55% of Rajasthan's mapped rock area.
Small Rajasthan slice of the Gujarat-dominant Sabarmati basin — Kotra (Udaipur) and southern Sirohi.
Reaches the Arabian Sea via Gujarat's Gulf of Khambhat.
Wakal + Sei confluence at Rani (Udaipur–Sirohi) is the basin's headwater.
Rises in the Aravalli near Kotra (Udaipur) at ~762 m elevation and flows southwest into Gujarat.
The Rajasthan reach is only ~45 km; the river is defined as originating from Wakal + Sei confluence at Rani in Sirohi/Udaipur.
One of only two Rajasthan rivers that reach the Arabian Sea (the other is the Mahi).
Ring sanctuary around Sajjangarh Palace (Monsoon Palace) west of Udaipur — notified 1987.
Rajasthan's smallest sanctuary at 5.19 sq km; a re-wilded fort landscape.
Successful sambar and blue bull reintroduction site; a birder's day-trip destination from Udaipur.
extraFacts_hi: उदयपुर के पश्चिम में सज्जनगढ़ महल (मानसून पैलेस) के चारों ओर वलय अभयारण्य — 1987 में अधिसूचित।
5.19 वर्ग किमी के साथ राजस्थान का सबसे छोटा अभयारण्य; एक पुनर्वनीकृत दुर्ग-भूदृश्य।
सांभर और नीलगाय के सफल पुनर्वास का स्थल; उदयपुर से पक्षी-प्रेमियों का एक-दिनी गंतव्य।

extraFacts: Carved out of Udaipur on 7 August 2023 — one of Rajasthan's smallest new districts by population.
Salumbar was the seat of a first-class Solanki Rajput thikana under Mewar; the Salumbar thikana's Rani Hadi is remembered for the Kunwar Chundawat war-tokens episode of 1657.
Jaisamand Lake (Salumbar) — built by Maharana Jai Singh in 1691 — is Rajasthan's second-largest artificial lake and one of the world's largest man-made lakes of its era.
The Aravalli spine here rises to Jarga peak (1431 m) — Rajasthan's fourth-highest peak.
Salumbar is Rajasthan's newest scheduled-area district under the Fifth Schedule — an extension of the Vagad tribal belt.
Ramsar site since 1990; India's largest inland salt lake at ~190 sq km.
Winter refuge for ~20,000 flamingoes and pelicans; 2019 saw mass die-offs due to avian botulism.
The lake produces ~9% of India's salt — Sambhar Salts Ltd (a Hindustan Salts + Rajasthan JV) is the operator.
extraFacts_hi: 1990 से रामसर स्थल; ~190 वर्ग किमी के साथ भारत की सबसे बड़ी अंतःस्थलीय लवण झील।
~20,000 राजहंस और पेलिकन की शीतकालीन शरण; 2019 में एवियन बोटुलिज़्म से सामूहिक मृत्यु हुई।
झील भारत का ~9% नमक देती है — संचालक Sambhar Salts Ltd. (Hindustan Salts + राजस्थान का संयुक्त उपक्रम)।

extraFacts: India's largest inland salt lake — 190 km² surface area, in Jaipur–Ajmer–Nagaur trijunction.
Ramsar site since 1990; produces ~9% of India's salt from evaporation ponds run by Sambhar Salts Ltd.
Winter refuge for over 20,000 flamingoes and pelicans; mass die-offs (2019) drew national attention to overdraft.
The salt was so central to trade that Sambhar was the historic Chauhan capital before Ajmer.
extraFacts_hi: भारत की सबसे बड़ी अंतःस्थलीय लवण झील — 190 वर्ग किमी सतही क्षेत्र, जयपुर–अजमेर–नागौर त्रिसंगम पर।
1990 से रामसर स्थल; Sambhar Salts Ltd. की वाष्पीकरण क्यारियों से भारत का ~9% नमक बनता है।
20,000 से अधिक राजहंस और पेलिकन की शीतकालीन शरण; 2019 की सामूहिक मृत्यु ने अति-दोहन पर राष्ट्रीय ध्यान खींचा।
नमक व्यापार में इतना केंद्रीय कि अजमेर से पहले सांभर चौहानों की ऐतिहासिक राजधानी था।

extraFacts: Rajasthan's second Tiger Reserve; sole Indian reserve where tigers were reintroduced after local extinction (2008 onwards).
Kankwari Fort inside the park was where Aurangzeb imprisoned his brother Dara Shikoh.
Ancient Bhartrihari temple, Neelkanth Mahadev ruins (6th c.), and Pandupol (Bhima's legendary spot) sit within the park.
extraFacts_hi: राजस्थान का दूसरा टाइगर रिज़र्व; भारत का एकमात्र रिज़र्व जहाँ स्थानीय विलुप्ति के बाद बाघों का पुनर्वास हुआ (2008 से)।
उद्यान के भीतर कांकवाड़ी दुर्ग वही स्थान है जहाँ औरंगज़ेब ने अपने भाई दारा शिकोह को क़ैद रखा था।
प्राचीन भर्तृहरि मंदिर, नीलकंठ महादेव के अवशेष (6वीं शताब्दी) और पांडुपोल (भीम से जुड़ा पौराणिक स्थल) उद्यान के भीतर हैं।

extraFacts: Notified 1978 — India's first case of a tiger population going locally extinct (2004) and being reintroduced (2008).
Second Tiger Reserve of Rajasthan; tigers here today all descend from Ranthambore founders.
Home to India's second-oldest Kankwari Fort and the Neelkanth Mahadev temple ruins (6th c.).
extraFacts_hi: 1978 में अधिसूचित — भारत का पहला मामला जहाँ बाघ-आबादी स्थानीय रूप से विलुप्त हुई (2004) और फिर पुनर्वासित की गई (2008)।
राजस्थान का दूसरा टाइगर रिज़र्व; यहाँ के सभी बाघ रणथंभौर के संस्थापक बाघों के वंशज हैं।
यहाँ भारत का दूसरा सबसे प्राचीन कांकवाड़ी दुर्ग और नीलकंठ महादेव मंदिर के अवशेष (6वीं शताब्दी) हैं।

extraFacts: The larger sanctuary boundary of Sariska National Park; notified 1955.
Includes buffer villages where tigers reintroduced from Ranthambore (2008 onwards) now range.
Home to the Neelkanth Mahadev temple ruins (6th c., Bala hills) and the abandoned Sariska Palace.
extraFacts_hi: सरिस्का राष्ट्रीय उद्यान की बड़ी अभयारण्य सीमा; 1955 में अधिसूचित।
इसमें वे बफ़र गाँव शामिल हैं जहाँ रणथंभौर से पुनर्वासित बाघ (2008 से) अब विचरण करते हैं।
यहाँ नीलकंठ महादेव मंदिर के अवशेष (6वीं शताब्दी, बाला पहाड़ियाँ) और परित्यक्त सरिस्का पैलेस हैं।

extraFacts: Ranthambhore National Park (Sawai Madhopur) was Rajasthan's first National Park (1980) and India's first Tiger Reserve under Project Tiger (1973).
Ranthambhore Fort (10th c., Chauhan) is one of the six Hill Forts of Rajasthan (UNESCO 2013) — resisted Alauddin Khilji's 1301 siege after a year of hunger.
Trinetra Ganesh Temple (Ranthambhore fort) is India's oldest and largest three-eyed Ganesh shrine; devotees send Ganesha wedding invitations from across India by post.
Rameshvaram (Khandar tahsil) is one of Rajasthan's three triveni sangams — where Chambal, Banas and Seep meet.
The Chauth Mata temple (Sawai Madhopur) is a Chauhan-era tutelary goddess shrine; the district's northern edge is the Chambal Ravines.
Buffer sanctuary of Ranthambore Tiger Reserve — notified 1984 in memory of Maharaja Sawai Man Singh II of Jaipur.
Corridor between Ranthambore National Park and the Kailadevi sanctuary — tiger dispersal happens through here.
Named for the last ruling maharaja of Jaipur (r. 1922-1949, d. 1970).
extraFacts_hi: रणथंभौर टाइगर रिज़र्व का बफ़र अभयारण्य — 1984 में जयपुर के महाराजा सवाई मानसिंह द्वितीय की स्मृति में अधिसूचित।
रणथंभौर राष्ट्रीय उद्यान और कैलादेवी अभयारण्य के बीच गलियारा — बाघों का फैलाव यहीं से होता है।
नाम जयपुर के अंतिम शासक महाराजा पर (शासन 1922-1949, निधन 1970)।

extraFacts: Sanctuary along the Parvan river in Baran district — notified 1983.
Once part of the Baran royal hunting grounds — leopard, sloth bear and hyena populations preserved.
Buffer to the Shahbad Sahariya PVTG territory; site of one of Rajasthan's oldest known irrigation weirs.
extraFacts_hi: बारां जिले में परवन नदी के किनारे अभयारण्य — 1983 में अधिसूचित।
कभी बारां के राजसी शिकार-क्षेत्र का भाग — तेंदुआ, भालू और लकड़बग्घा की आबादी संरक्षित।
शाहबाद सहरिया PVTG क्षेत्र का बफ़र; राजस्थान के प्राचीनतम ज्ञात सिंचाई बंधों में से एक यहीं है।

extraFacts: State's first hi-tech district (declared 2019) — anchor of the Shekhawati fresco belt with Laxmangarh, Fatehpur and Ramgarh Sethan.
Sam Gau Mata Mandir at Rewasa (Datarangamgarh tahsil, Sikar) is India's fourth Gau Mata Temple.
Jagmalpura (Sikar) is Rajasthan's only NCC Gram (National Cadet Corps village) — where the entire able-bodied youth is NCC-enrolled.
Rajasthan's first Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) was established at Fatehpur Shekhawati in 1976.
Khatushyamji (Sikar) — the Khatu-Shyam Krishna avatar — is Rajasthan's third-biggest Vaishnavite pilgrimage after Nathdwara and Sanwaliya Seth.
The Shri Saraswati Pustakalaya at Fatehpur holds one of Rajasthan's largest collections of rare and unattainable literature.
Home to Rajasthan's only hill station — Mount Abu (1220 m).
Guru Shikhar (1722 m) in Sirohi is the highest point of the Aravalli and of Rajasthan.
Dilwara Temples (Mount Abu) — five 11th-13th c. white-marble Jain temples of the Solanki era — considered India's finest marble carving.
India's first Eco-Sensitive Zone was declared here as 'Aburaj' (formerly Mount Abu); Rajasthan's first British-era municipality was Abu (1864).
Achalgarh Fort near Mount Abu holds the Achaleshwar Mahadev inscription — a key medieval Paramara epigraphic source.
Sirohi's Kailash Sanhita (Achalgarh) shrine is one of only 12 in India where the shivling has been continuously worshipped for over 5000 years by tradition.
Pratapgarh–Chittorgarh–Udaipur trijunction sanctuary — notified 1979.
India's densest Indian flying squirrel (Petaurista philippensis) habitat; also hosts the Rajasthan state animal, chinkara.
Named for Sita — legend places her Valmiki-ashram exile in these forests where the twins Luv and Kush were born.
extraFacts_hi: प्रतापगढ़–चित्तौड़गढ़–उदयपुर त्रिसंगम अभयारण्य — 1979 में अधिसूचित।
भारत का सघनतम उड़न गिलहरी (Petaurista philippensis) आवास; राजस्थान का राज्य-पशु चिंकारा भी यहाँ है।
नाम सीता पर — किंवदंती उनके वाल्मीकि-आश्रम वनवास को इन्हीं वनों में रखती है, जहाँ लव-कुश का जन्म हुआ।

extraFacts: Entisols/Inceptisols — river-borne sediments of the Banas, Chambal and Yamuna sub-basins.
Highly fertile; supports the wheat-mustard rotation of the eastern plains + Bharatpur-Alwar belt.
Requires organic manure to sustain productivity under intensive cropping.
Vertisols — self-mulching clay derived from Deccan-trap basalt weathering.
Highly retentive of moisture — supports rain-fed soybean and gram in the Hadoti plateau.
Cracks deeply in summer; wheat, cotton and soybean rotate here without irrigation.
Aridisols — sandy loam with low organic matter (<0.5%), high pH (8-9).
Suited to drought-hardy crops: bajra, moth, guar. Needs sub-surface irrigation for wheat / cotton.
Erosion hazard: severe wind erosion; dunes stabilised by khejri (Prosopis cineraria) shelterbelts.
Transitional soils in the Bhilwara–Ajmer–Tonk arc between Aravalli piedmont and Vindhyan foreslope.
Higher fertility than pure red loamy; supports wheat, gram, mustard under partial irrigation.
Groundwater draft is Rajasthan's second-highest in this zone (after Sri Ganganagar).
Alfisols — moderate-fertility soils on Aravalli piedmonts (Ajmer, Bhilwara, Udaipur).
Supports rainfed maize, gram, groundnut, mustard; the tribal maize belt sits on these soils.
Iron-oxide gives red colour; low water-holding capacity requires supplementary irrigation for wheat.
'Kallar' or 'reh' soils — saline-alkaline patches near Sambhar, Pachpadra, Didwana salt basins.
Highly restrictive; salt-tolerant crops (dhainch, barley) are the only sustainable option.
Reclamation via gypsum + green manure has recovered ~50,000 ha in the Sambhar catchment since the 1980s.
Aravalli hill Entisols — shallow, stony, low water-holding capacity.
Support natural thorn scrub and dhok (Anogeissus pendula) forests, not cropping.
Erosion-prone; the Kumbhalgarh and Sariska WLS forests grow on skeletal soils.
Broke the ₹3/kWh solar tariff barrier in the 2017 auction, hitting ₹2.44/kWh.
Ownership shared across ~10 solar power developers (SBG Cleantech, Acme, ReNew, Adani, Azure, Hero Future Energies).
A right-bank tributary of the Mahi in the tribal Vagad belt.
Rises in the Beecha Meecha hills (Udaipur) and joins the Mahi at Beneshwar — Rajasthan's biggest tribal pilgrimage.
Feeds the Som-Kamla-Amba dam system supporting Dungarpur irrigation.
Hadoti plateau (Kota, Bundi, Baran, Jhalawar) — Deccan Trap-edge and Vindhyan sandstone tableland.
Elevation 300-500 m; Vertisol (black cotton) soils support soybean and gram.
The Chambal cuts through in a 100 m deep gorge — India's cleanest large river passes through here.
Southern Aravalli + Mahi basin — Udaipur, Banswara, Dungarpur, Pratapgarh, Salumbar.
Rajasthan's most rugged relief and highest rainfall (700-1000 mm).
The tribal Vagad belt sits here; anchors the Mahi's headwaters and the Bhil demographic majority.
Highest SC share in Rajasthan (36.6%) — the canal-command labour communities.
'Grain bowl' of Rajasthan — wheat, mustard, kinnow, cotton — driven by IGNP (Indira Gandhi Nahar Pariyojana) + Gang Canal.
Bounded by Punjab (north) and Pakistan (west) — Rajasthan's northernmost district and one of only two touching Punjab.
The Gang Canal (1927) is the oldest large irrigation canal in Rajasthan — the district's founding infrastructure.
Sri Ganganagar is the state's westernmost point of the Bhakra system; IGNP's Suratgarh Super Thermal Power Station is Rajasthan's first super thermal power plant.
Rajasthan Punjabi Language Academy (2006) — the state's only Punjabi language academy — is headquartered in Sri Ganganagar.
A left-bank tributary of the Luni in the Marwar arid belt.
Rises in the Aravalli near Deosa (Sirohi/Pali) and flows northwest through Jalore.
One of Rajasthan's few 'living' Luni-system tributaries thanks to Aravalli piedmont springs.
Grassland sanctuary in Churu — notified 1962; India's densest blackbuck (Antilope cervicapra) habitat outside Velavadar.
'Chhapar' means a flat saline depression — the sanctuary's unique ecosystem is a saline flat grassland.
Also Rajasthan's premier winter raptor site — hosts eastern imperial eagle, Tawny eagle, harriers and short-toed snake eagles in migration.
extraFacts_hi: चूरू का घास-मैदान अभयारण्य — 1962 में अधिसूचित; वेलावदर के बाहर भारत का सघनतम कृष्णमृग (Antilope cervicapra) आवास।
'छापर' का अर्थ है समतल लवणीय गर्त — अभयारण्य का विशिष्ट पारितंत्र लवणीय समतल घास-मैदान है।
राजस्थान का प्रमुख शीतकालीन शिकारी-पक्षी स्थल भी — प्रवास में ईस्टर्न इम्पीरियल ईगल, टॉनी ईगल, हैरियर और शॉर्ट-टोड स्नेक ईगल आते हैं।

extraFacts: Summer max: 45-50°C (Phalodi recorded India's highest reliable air temp of 51.0°C on 19 May 2016).
Winter min: 0-3°C; frost is common in Churu.
Diurnal range often exceeds 20°C — the widest daily temperature swing of any Indian climate zone.
Summer max: 42-46°C; winter min: 3-8°C.
Churu regularly holds the state's twin records — highest summer AND lowest winter temperature in the same year.
The 'hot-summer semi-arid' zone anchors Rajasthan's outdoor evaporation industries — salt production and dyeing.
Jhalawar–Banswara–Pratapgarh — Rajasthan's mildest zone; summer max 38-42°C, winter min 10-14°C.
Monsoon-moderated temperatures; humidity remains 60-80% for four months a year.
Malwa plateau's black cotton soils (Vertisols) hold moisture through the winter, buffering diurnal swings.
Aravalli elevation moderates the arid extremes — summer max 38-42°C, winter min 8-12°C.
Mount Abu (Sirohi) is the coolest station in Rajasthan — summer max 32°C, winter min occasionally sub-zero.
The Sabarmati and Mahi headwater catchments sit in this zone — high rainfall on cool Aravalli slopes.
Covers ~60% of Rajasthan — the state's largest physiographic region.
Aeolian sand cover with longitudinal, star, and barchan dunes; playa lakes (rann) concentrate salt.
Elevation gradient from 100-300 m; Rajasthan's lowest point (Bap, Phalodi, ~100 m) sits here.
Aravalli-ridge sanctuary spanning Ajmer, Pali and Rajsamand — notified 1983.
Buffer to the Kumbhalgarh–Beawar Aravalli corridor; leopard, sloth bear and hyena populations.
Named for Colonel James Tod (1782-1835), the East India Company political agent who wrote 'Annals and Antiquities of Rajasthan'.
extraFacts_hi: अजमेर, पाली और राजसमंद में फैला अरावली-कटक अभयारण्य — 1983 में अधिसूचित।
कुंभलगढ़–ब्यावर अरावली गलियारे का बफ़र; तेंदुआ, भालू और लकड़बग्घा की आबादी।
नाम कर्नल जेम्स टॉड (1782-1835) पर, जिन्होंने 'Annals and Antiquities of Rajasthan' लिखी।

extraFacts: Rajasthan's only Muslim princely state — founded 1817 by Amir Khan Pindari with a British sanad.
Bisalpur Dam (Deoli tahsil, Tonk) on the Banas — Rajasthan's largest drinking-water reservoir; supplies Jaipur, Ajmer and Tonk via a 144-km pipeline.
Central Sheep and Wool Research Institute (Avikanagar, Malpura tahsil, Tonk) established 1962 — India's premier woolen-fibre research hub.
Home to Rasoolpura (Rani Sahiba Library / Maulana Azad Arabic-Persian Research Institute) — one of India's largest collections of Persian and Arabic manuscripts.
Rajmahal (Tonk) is one of Rajasthan's three triveni sangams — where Dai, Khari and Banas rivers meet.
Tonk district was Rajasthan's first to issue a family-photograph BPL card, and holds the Chauhan-era historic town of Uniara.
Founded 1559 by Maharana Udai Singh II after Chittor's third sack — the Mewar capital for the next four centuries.
Sits in the Girwa valley — a natural amphitheatre bowl in the southern Aravalli that made the site defensible.
'City of Lakes' — Pichola, Fateh Sagar, Swaroop Sagar, Rangsagar and Doodh Talai are five interconnected artificial lakes commissioned across five centuries.
Jaisamand Lake (built 1691, Maharana Jai Singh) — Rajasthan's second-largest artificial lake and one of the world's largest man-made lakes of its era.
Zawar (Girva tahsil, Udaipur) — Asia's largest lead-zinc mine complex and India's oldest known zinc-smelting site (12th c. distillation retorts, world-first industrial zinc production).
Home to IIM Udaipur (2011) — the only IIM in Rajasthan — and Rajasthan's oldest folk-culture research centre, Bharatiya Lok Kala Mandal (1952, Devi Lal Samar).
Bagor Haveli holds the world's largest turban (pagdi) — 850 m long, in the museum's collection.
Dholpur river-forest sanctuary — notified 1955, one of the oldest in Rajasthan.
Chambal Ravines habitat — chinkara, chausingha (four-horned antelope), blue bull.
Adjacent to the Ramsagar sanctuary; the two form the northern Chambal Sanctuary buffer.
extraFacts_hi: धौलपुर का नदी-वन अभयारण्य — 1955 में अधिसूचित, राजस्थान के प्राचीनतम में से एक।
चंबल बीहड़ आवास — चिंकारा, चौसिंगा, नीलगाय।
रामसागर अभयारण्य से सटा; दोनों मिलकर उत्तरी चंबल अभयारण्य का बफ़र बनाते हैं।

extraFacts: Champion & Seth Type 5A — Southern Tropical Dry Deciduous; covers Aravalli piedmonts and Vindhyan slopes.
Key species: dhok (Anogeissus pendula), salar (Boswellia serrata), tendu, palash, mahua.
Ranthambore, Kumbhalgarh and Sariska tiger habitats are dry-deciduous.
Mixed thorn–deciduous ecotone across central Rajasthan (Jodhpur, Nagaur, Ajmer, Tonk).
Rohira (Tecomella undulata — 'marwar teak'), khejri, and neem dominate; dhok on rocky spurs.
The Rajasthan state flower (rohira) is native to this ecotone.
Tal Chhapar and Sonkhaliya (Ajmer) grasslands host India's densest blackbuck and Great Indian Bustard remnants respectively.
Dominant grasses: sewan (Lasiurus scindicus), dhaman (Cenchrus setigerus), murath — the Thar's key fodder base.
The Great Indian Bustard (Ardeotis nigriceps) — Rajasthan's state bird — survives only in these grasslands.
Champion & Seth Type 8A — Northern Subtropical Semi-Evergreen; only on Mount Abu (Sirohi).
Key species: pipal, banyan, banj oak, jamun, wild mango; Rajasthan's only orchid populations.
Habitat of the endemic Mount Abu skink (Eurylepis moerella) and India's westernmost leopard-native evergreen population.
Riverine forests along the Chambal, Banas, Mahi and Luni; jamun, arjun, khejri belts.
The National Chambal Sanctuary's forests are Rajasthan's largest surviving riparian tract.
Home to smooth-coated otter, mugger crocodile and gharial — riparian mammals and reptiles.
Champion & Seth Type 6B/C1 — Northern Tropical Thorn Forest; dominant in Barmer, Bikaner, Churu, Jaisalmer.
Key species: khejri (Prosopis cineraria — Rajasthan's state tree), rohira, kair, ker, phog, jaal.
Sacred groves ('orans') protect these forests around village common lands — Bhadria Oran (17,804 ha, Jaisalmer) is India's largest.
Fringing vegetation of Keoladeo, Sambhar, Pushkar, Bisalpur — reeds, sedges, water lilies, hydrilla.
Keoladeo hosts India's densest painted-stork breeding colony.
Rajasthan's Ramsar sites (Keoladeo 1981, Sambhar 1990, Khichan 2023, Menar 2023) protect four distinct wetland vegetation types.
Distinct from the Chambal-basin Banas — rises in the Aravalli at Sirohi and flows southwest into Gujarat's Little Rann.
One of only two Rajasthan rivers that reach the Arabian Sea; the other is the Mahi.
Its Gujarat lower course is dammed at Dantiwada — Rajasthan's contribution is monsoon flow only.

images.caption: Shrinathji shringar on Vitthalnathji's birthday — a seasonal Pichwai theme.
Karni Mata temple at Deshnoke, Bikaner — the 'rat temple' housing ~25,000 kabbas.
Sachiya Mata Temple at Osian, Jodhpur — 8th c. Pratihara sandstone shikhara.
Chittorgarh Fort — India's largest fort by area (280 hectares); UNESCO Hill Fort of Rajasthan.
Kumbhalgarh Fort — 36 km walls, world's second-longest continuous fortification.
Amber Fort, Amer — Kachhawaha capital before Jaipur (1727); UNESCO Hill Fort of Rajasthan.
Mehrangarh Fort, Jodhpur — Rao Jodha's 1459 seat of the Rathores, rising 122 m above the city.
Jaisalmer Fort — Rajasthan's only 'living fort', yellow-sandstone Bhati capital (1156).
